revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Amidaji Kofun Cluster]] {{Short description| part_of of Yayoi period Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Amidaji Kofun cluster | native_name =阿弥大寺古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tottori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Amidaji Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|24|N|133|45|13|E|display=inline, title|qq=}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|en|Kurayoshi, Tottori|qid=Q642006|lt=Kurayoshi, Tottori|1=Kurayoshi, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 3rd century AD | abandoned = | epochs = {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|25|24|N|133|45|13|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Amidaji Kofun cluster'''|阿弥大寺古墳群|||qq=}} is a group of three late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Shimofukuda neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|en|Kurayoshi, Tottori|qid=Q642006|lt=Kurayoshi|1=Kurayoshi, Tottori}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|qid=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|qid=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1981. <ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/210950|title= 阿弥大寺古墳群|trans-title= Amidaji Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Amidaji Kofun cluster is located on the side of a hill overlooking Kokubugawa River. The cluster consists of three {{nihongo|''Yosumi-tosshutsu-gata''|四隅突出形|qq=}}-style tumuli. This is a variation of the {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=}}-style square tumulus with polygonal-shaped protrusions on each of its four corners. This style is unique to the {{ill|Kibi Province|en|Kibi Province|qid=Q1155128|lt=Kibi|1=Kingdom of Kibi}}, San'in and {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}}s of Japan. Kofun No. 1 is about 17.8 meters long, including protrusions, Kofun No. 2 is 8.8 meters long, and Kofun No. 3 is 7.8 meters long. Parts of the slopes and protrusions were protected by river stones, similar to ''[[fukiishi]]''. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were conducted in 1979 and 1980. {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|qid=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}}, both ceremonial and for everyday use, was found around the protruding portions, but the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were not excavated. Currently, the site is backfilled for preservation, and the excavated artifacts are stored in the Kurayoshi Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The site is located a ten-minute walk from "Kamifukuda" bus stop on the Hinomaru Bus from {{ill|Kurayoshi Station|en|Kurayoshi Station|qid=Q899254|lt=Kurayoshi Station|1=Kurayoshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|qid=Q6573288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tottori)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/040B474FAAC9B26E4925796F0007FCE9?OpenDocument Cultural properties of Tottori] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.city.kurayoshi.lg.jp/gyousei/div/kyouiku/bunkazai/1/1/3/ Kurayoshi City home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} Amidaji Kofun Cluster
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Amidaji Kofun Cluster]]
